You are running as a single automated phase of the ADW pipeline.
The orchestrator performs ALL git and GitHub work for this run: do NOT run git or gh, do NOT create/switch/commit/push branches, and do NOT open, merge, or comment on pull requests. If the task section below tells you to do any of that, skip those steps.
You have no GitHub access in this phase; all issue context you need is provided inline.

---

Classify work item #3 into a single change type for branch naming and commit prefixing.

Work item context (title, body, labels):

3 GitHub issue #3: feat: MVP live-run observation ledger
Labels: issue_class:feat adw-live-batch

Part of the MVP live-run batch (`adw_sdlc/docs/LIVE-RUN-BATCH.md`). Intended `issue_class`: **feat**. Planned ADW run mode: native.

## Goal
Add an "observed-live" ledger so each `MVP-READINESS.md` parity guarantee can be marked as seen in a real run, not only under mocks.

## Scope
- New `adw_sdlc/docs/OBSERVED-LIVE-LEDGER.md` with a row per PARITY.md guarantee: guarantee | mocked? | observed-live? | run id / evidence.
- Seed it from the existing live `claude` run (PR #331) where applicable.
- Link it from `MVP-READINESS.md` §1.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Ledger lists every PARITY.md Section-10 guarantee.
- [ ] Each row has an explicit observed-live status.
- [ ] Linked from MVP-READINESS.md.
- [ ] `npm run verify` stays green.

## Notes
Docs/feature artifact; no kernel change required.

## Instructions

- Choose exactly one `issue_class` from: `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `chore`, `ci`, `test`, `refactor`.
  - `feat` — new user-visible feature or capability.
  - `fix` — bug fix or correctness change.
  - `docs` — documentation-only change.
  - `chore` — maintenance, deps, tooling with no user-visible behavior change.
  - `ci` — CI/build/workflow change.
  - `test` — test-only change.
  - `refactor` — internal restructuring with no behavior change.
- Prefer the narrowest truthful classification.
- If labels and body disagree, classify by the actual requested change.
